You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the OpenAirInterface 5G codebase.
You have access to the following tools to navigate and modify the codebase:

1. **bash** - Execute shell commands. Use for: grep, find, ls, gcc -fsyntax-only, etc.
2. **str_replace_editor** - View and edit files. Commands:
   - view: View file contents (with optional line range)
   - str_replace: Replace a specific string in a file
   - create: Create a new file
   - insert: Insert text at a specific line

The codebase is a 5G telecommunications implementation with PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, RRC layers.
Working directory is /repo.

You will be given a pull request description. Your task is to implement the changes
described in the PR. Navigate the codebase, understand the relevant code, and make
the necessary modifications.

When you are done making your changes, output SUBMIT to indicate you are finished.

Think step by step. First understand what the PR is asking for, then locate the relevant
code, and finally implement the changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Fix race condition in PDN disconnect procedure between timer expiry and message processing

### Summary
The PDN disconnect procedure in the NAS UE layer had a race condition between the T3492 retransmission timer expiry handler and the processing of PDN disconnect reject messages. When a reject message arrived while the timer was expiring, both code paths would concurrently access shared resources including the retransmission counter and procedure transaction state. This caused inconsistent state updates, potential memory corruption, and intermittent crashes in the UE NAS layer.

This fix introduces a mutex to serialize access to these shared resources. The timer handler and message processing function now acquire the same lock before modifying any shared state, eliminating the race condition. The mutex is implemented as a static initializer to ensure proper cleanup and avoid dynamic allocation.

### Changes
- `openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/PdnDisconnect.c`: 
  - Added static `pdn_disconnect_mutex` with `PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER`
  - Wrapped timer stop, state changes, and bearer context modifications in `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` with mutex lock/unlock
  - Wrapped retransmission counter increment and timeout processing in `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` with mutex lock/unlock
  - Ensured all exit paths properly release the mutex

### Testing
- Verified the fix through code inspection and static analysis to confirm all shared resource access paths are protected
- Ran ThreadSanitizer on the NAS UE component under simulated network conditions; no data race warnings reported in the PDN disconnect procedure
- Validated normal PDN connectivity establishment and release operations continue to work correctly with no functional regression

I'll help you implement this fix for the race condition in the PDN disconnect procedure.     39	        disconnection from one PDN.
    40

    42	        ding the default EPS bearer context, are released.

... [truncated] ...
, pid);
   127	    } else if (esm_data->pdn[pid].data == NULL) {
   128	      LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-PROC  - PDN connection %d has not been "
   129	                "allocated", pid);
   130	    } else if (!esm_data->pdn[pid].is_active) {
   131	      LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PDN connection is not active");
   132	    } else {
   133	      /* Get the procedure transaction identity assigned to the PDN
   134	       * connection to be released */
   135	      *pti = esm_data->pdn[pid].data->pti;
   136	      /* Get the EPS bearer identity of the default bearer associated
   137	       * with the PDN to disconnect from */
   138	      *ebi = esm_data->pdn[pid].data->bearer[0]->ebi;
   139	      rc = RETURNok;
   140	    }
   141	  }
   142	
   143	  LOG_FUNC_RETURN(rc);
   144	}
   145	
   146	/****************************************************************************
   147	 **                                                                        **
   148	 ** Name:    esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_request()                         **
   149	 **                                                                        **
   150	 ** Description: Initiates PDN disconnection procedure in order to request **
   151	 **      disconnection from a PDN.                                 **
   152	 **                                                                        **
   153	 **              3GPP TS 24.301, section 6.5.2.2                           **
   154	 **      The UE requests PDN disconnection from a PDN by sending a **
   155	 **      PDN DISCONNECT REQUEST message to the MME, starting timer **
   156	 **      T3492 and entering state PROCEDURE TRANSACTION PENDING.   **
   157	 **                                                                        **
   158	 ** Inputs:  is_standalone: Should be always true                      **
   159	 **      pti:       Procedure transaction identity             **
   160	 **      msg:       Encoded PDN disconnect request message to  **
   161	 **             be sent                                    **
   162	 **      sent_by_ue:    Not used                                   **
   163	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   164	 **                                                                        **
   165	 ** Outputs:     None                                                      **
   166	 **      Return:    RETURNok, RETURNerror                      **
   167	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   168	 **                                                                        **
   169	 ***************************************************************************/
   170	int esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_request(nas_user_t *user, bool is_standalone, int pti,
   171	                                    OctetString *msg, bool sent_by_ue)
   172	{
   173	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   174	
   175	  int rc = RETURNok;
   176	  esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data = user->esm_pt_data;
   177	  LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-PROC  - Initiate PDN disconnection (pti=%d)", pti);
   178	
   179	  if (is_standalone) {
   180	    emm_sap_t emm_sap;
   181	    emm_esm_data_t *emm_esm = &emm_sap.u.emm_esm.u.data;
   182	    /*
   183	     * Notity EMM that ESM PDU has to be forwarded to lower layers
   184	     */
   185	    emm_sap.primitive = EMMESM_UNITDATA_REQ;
   186	    emm_sap.u.emm_esm.ueid = user->ueid;
   187	    emm_esm->msg.length = msg->length;
   188	    emm_esm->msg.value = msg->value;
   189	    rc = emm_sap_send(user, &emm_sap);
   190	
   191	    if (rc != RETURNerror) {
   192	      /* Start T3482 retransmission timer */
   193	      rc = esm_pt_start_timer(user, pti, msg, T3492_DEFAULT_VALUE,
   194	                              _pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler);
   195	    }
   196	  }
   197	
   198	  if (rc != RETURNerror) {
   199	    /* Set the procedure transaction state to PENDING */
   200	    rc = esm_pt_set_status(esm_pt_data, pti, ESM_PT_PENDING);

Good, I found the file. Now let me analyze it to understand the race condition issue. Looking at the PR description, I need to:

1. Add a mutex to protect shared resources in the PDN disconnect procedure
2. The race condition is between:
   - `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` function (processing PDN disconnect reject messages)
   - `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` function (T3492 timer expiry handler)

Let me examine these two functions more closely to understand what shared resources they access:

From `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` (around line 260-330):
- Stops timer T3492: `esm_pt_stop_timer(user->esm_pt_data, pti)`
- Sets procedure transaction state: `esm_pt_set_status(user->esm_pt_data, pti, ESM_PT_INACTIVE)`
- Releases transaction identity: `esm_pt_release(user->esm_pt_data, pti)`
- Gets default EPS bearer identity: `_pdn_disconnect_get_default_ebi(esm_data, pti)`
- Sends ESM SAP message: `esm_sap_send(user, &esm_sap)`

From `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` (around line 360-430):
- Increments retransmission counter: `data->count += 1`
- Stops/starts timer T3492: `esm_pt_stop_timer()`, `esm_pt_start_timer()`
- Sets procedure transaction state: `esm_pt_set_status(user->esm_pt_data, data->pti, ESM_PT_INACTIVE)`
- Releases transaction identity: `esm_pt_release(user->esm_pt_data, data->pti)`
- Gets default EPS bearer identity: `_pdn_disconnect_get_default_ebi(esm_data, data->pti)`


I'll focus on implementing a robust synchronization mechanism to prevent concurrent access to critical sections. The key is to create a static mutex that protects these shared resources, ensuring thread-safe operations during PDN disconnect procedures. I'll strategically place lock and unlock calls to cover all critical code paths, preventing potential race conditions and maintaining data integrity.

The implementation requires careful placement of mutex operations, covering timer management, state transitions, and resource access. I'll ensure comprehensive protection across all potential concurrent access points, creating a reliable synchronization approach for the PDN disconnect workflow.

1	#ifndef _ESM_PT_DEFS_H
     2	#define _ESM_PT_DEFS_H
     3	
     4	#include "UTIL/nas_timer.h"
     5	#include "IES/ProcedureTransactionIdentity.h"
     6	
     7	/****************************************************************************/
     8	/*********************  G L O B A L    C O N S T A N T S  *******************/
     9	/****************************************************************************/
    10	
    11	/*
    12	 * Minimal and maximal value of a procedure transaction identity:
    13	 * The Procedure Transaction Identity (PTI) identifies bi-directional
    14	 * messages flows
    15	 */
    16	#define ESM_PTI_MIN     (PROCEDURE_TRANSACTION_IDENTITY_FIRST)
    17	#define ESM_PTI_MAX     (PROCEDURE_TRANSACTION_IDENTITY_LAST)
    18	
    19	/****************************************************************************/
    20	/************************  G L O B A L    T Y P E S  ************************/
    21	/****************************************************************************/
    22	
    23	/* Procedure transaction states */
    24	typedef enum {
    25	  ESM_PT_INACTIVE,    /* No procedure transaction exists      */
    26	  ESM_PT_PENDING, /* The UE has initiated a procedure transaction
    27	             * towards the network              */
    28	  ESM_PT_STATE_MAX
    29	} esm_pt_state;
    30	
    31	/* ESM message timer retransmission data */
    32	typedef struct {
    33	  unsigned char pti;      /* Procedure transaction identity   */
    34	  unsigned int count;     /* Retransmission counter       */
    35	  OctetString msg;        /* Encoded ESM message to re-transmit   */
    36	  void *user;             /* user reference - void to avoid cyclic dependency */
    37	} esm_pt_timer_data_t;
    38	
    39	/*
    40	 * --------------------------
    41	 * Procedure transaction data
    42	 * --------------------------
    43	 */
    44	typedef struct {
    45	  unsigned char pti;      /* Procedure transaction identity   */
    46	  esm_pt_state status;    /* Procedure transaction status     */
    47	  struct nas_timer_t timer;   /* Retransmission timer         */
    48	  esm_pt_timer_data_t *args;  /* Retransmission timer parameters data */
    49	} esm_pt_context_t;
    50	
    51	/*
    52	 * ------------------------------
    53	 * List of procedure transactions
    54	 * ------------------------------
    55	 */
    56	typedef struct {
    57	  unsigned char index;    /* Index of the next procedure transaction
    58	                 * identity to be used */
    59	#define ESM_PT_DATA_SIZE (ESM_PTI_MAX - ESM_PTI_MIN + 1)
    60	  esm_pt_context_t *context[ESM_PT_DATA_SIZE + 1];
    61	} esm_pt_data_t;
    62	
    63	#endif

/*****************************************************************************
Source      PdnConnectivity.c

Version     0.1

Date        2013/01/02

Product     NAS stack

Subsystem   EPS Session Management

Author      Frederic Maurel

Description Defines the PDN connectivity ESM procedure executed by the
        Non-Access Stratum.

        The PDN connectivity procedure is used by the UE to request
        the setup of a default EPS bearer to a PDN.

        The procedure is used either to establish the 1st default
        bearer by including the PDN CONNECTIVITY REQUEST message
        into the initial attach message, or to establish subsequent
        default bearers to additional PDNs in order to allow the UE
        simultaneous access to multiple PDNs by sending the message
        stand-alone.

*****************************************************************************/

#include <stdlib.h> // malloc, free

reasoning_content: Good, PdnConnectivity.c includes <stdlib.h>, so I can see that system headers are included. Now let me check if there are any other files that use pthread mutex in the NAS UE directory:  <|tool_calls_section_begin|> <|tool_call_begin|>  functions.bash:7  <|tool_call_argument_begin|>   {"command": "find /repo/openair3/NAS/UE -name \"*.c\" -exec grep -l \"pthread_mutex\" {} \\;"} <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

content: Exit code: 0

reasoning_content:
Good, so the PdnConnectivity.c file includes stdlib.h, which is a good sign that we can add other standard headers like pthread.h. Now let me understand the race condition issue:

1. The race condition is between:
   - `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` - processes PDN disconnect reject messages
   - `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` - handles T3492 timer expiry

2. Both access shared resources including:
   - Retransmission counter (`data->count`)
   - Procedure transaction state (`esm_pt_set_status`, `esm_pt_release`)
   - Bearer context modifications

Let me examine the exact functions to understand what needs to be protected:

From the file view:
1. `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` - lines 209-287
2. `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` - lines 310-409

Now let me implement the fix according to the PR description:

1. Add a static mutex with `PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER`
2. Wrap the critical sections in `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` with mutex lock/unlock
3. Wrap the critical sections in `_pdn_disconnect_t3492_handler()` with mutex lock/unlock
4. Ensure all exit paths properly release the mutex

Let me start implementing:

Now I need to protect the `esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject()` function with the mutex. Let me look at the current implementation:   , pid);
   127    } else if (esm_data->pdn[pid].data == NULL) {
   128      LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-PROC  - PDN connection %d has not been "
   129                "allocated", pid);
   130    } else if (!esm_data->pdn[pid].is_active) {
   131      LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PDN connection is not active");
   132    } else {
   133      /* Get the procedure transaction identity assigned to the PDN
   134       * connection to be released */
   135      *pti = esm_data->pdn[pid].data->pti;
   136      /* Get the EPS bearer identity of the default bearer associated
   137       * with the PDN to disconnect from */

   139      rc = RETURNok;

   142
   143  LOG_FUNC_RETURN(rc);

   145
   146  /****************************************************************************
   147   **                                                                        **
   148   ** Name:    esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_request()                         **
   149   **                                                                        **
   150   ** Description: Initiates PDN disconnection procedure in order to request **
   151   **      disconnection from a PDN.                                 **
   152   **                                                                        **
   153   **              3GPP TS 24.301, section 6.5.2.2                           **
   154   **      The UE requests PDN disconnection from a PDN by sending a **
   155   **      PDN DISCONNECT REQUEST message to the MME, starting timer **
   156   **      T3492 and entering state PROCEDURE TRANSACTION PENDING.   **
   157   **                                                                        **
   158   ** Inputs:  is_standalone: Should be always true                      **
   159   **      pti:       Procedure transaction identity             **
   160   **      msg:       Encoded PDN disconnect request message to  **
   161   **             be sent                                    **
   162   **      sent_by_ue:    Not used                                   **
   163   **      Others:    None                                       **
   164   **                                                                        **
   165   ** Outputs:     None                                                      **
   166   **      Return:    RETURNok, RETURNerror                      **
   167   **      Others:    None                                       **
   168   **                                                                        **
   169   ***************************************************************************/

I notice the function is handling PDN connection validation, checking for various error conditions like invalid PDN connection index, NULL data, and inactive status. When a valid PDN connection is found, it retrieves the procedure transaction identity and EPS bearer identity for disconnection. The logic ensures proper error handling and provides informative logging throughout the process.

reasoning_content: Let me view the exact content of the function to match it correctly:   <|tool_calls_section_begin|>   <|tool_call_begin|>   functions.str_replace_editor:10  <|tool_call_argument_begin|>   {"command": "view", "path": "/repo/openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/PdnDisconnect.c", "view_range": [240, 280]}  <|tool_call_end|>   <|tool_calls_section_end|>

<|tool_calls_section_begin|>   <|tool_call_begin|>   functions.str_replace_editor:10  <|tool_call_argument_begin|>   {"command": "str_replace", "path": "/repo/openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/PdnDisconnect.c", "old_str": "int esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject(nas_user_t *user, int pti, int *esm_cause)
{
  LOG_FUNC_IN;
  esm_data_t *esm_data = user->esm_data;
  int rc;

  LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PDN disconnection rejected by the network "
            "(pti=%d), ESM cause = %d", pti, *esm_cause);

  /* Stop T3492 timer if running */
  (void) esm_pt_stop_timer(user->esm_pt_data, pti);
  /* Set the procedure transaction state to INACTIVE */
  rc = esm_pt_set_status(user->esm_pt_data, pti, ESM_PT_INACTIVE);

  if (rc != RETURNok) {
    /* The procedure transaction was already in INACTIVE state
     * as the request may have already been rejected */
    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PTI %d was already INACTIVE", pti);
    *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_MESSAGE_TYPE_NOT_COMPATIBLE;
  } else {
    /* Release the transaction identity assigned to this procedure */
    rc = esm_pt_release(user->esm_pt_data, pti);

    if (rc != RETURNok) {
      LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - Failed to release PTI %d", pti);
      *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_REQUEST_REJECTED_UNSPECIFIED;
    } else if (*esm_cause != ESM_CAUSE_LAST_PDN_DISCONNECTION_NOT_ALLOWED) {
      /* Get the identity of the default EPS bearer context allocated to
       * the PDN connection entry assigned to this procedure transaction */
      int ebi = _pdn_disconnect_get_default_ebi(esm_data, pti);

      if (ebi < 0) {
        LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-PROC  - No default EPS bearer found");
        *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_PROTOCOL_ERROR;
        LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
      }

      /*
       * Notify ESM that all EPS bearer contexts to this PDN have to be
       * locally deactivated
       */
      esm_sap_t esm_sap;
      esm_sap.primitive = ESM_EPS_BEARER_CONTEXT_DEACTIVATE_REQ;
      esm_sap.is_standalone = true;
      esm_sap.recv = NULL;
      esm_sap.send.length = 0;
      esm_sap.data.eps_bearer_context_deactivate.ebi = ebi;
      rc = esm_sap_send(user, &esm_sap);

      if (rc != RETURNok) {
        *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_PROTOCOL_ERROR;
      }
    }
  }

  LOG_FUNC_RETURN(rc);
}", "new_str": "int esm_proc_pdn_disconnect_reject(nas_user_t *user, int pti, int *esm_cause)
{
  LOG_FUNC_IN;
  esm_data_t *esm_data = user->esm_data;
  int rc;

  LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PDN disconnection rejected by the network "
            "(pti=%d), ESM cause = %d", pti, *esm_cause);

  /* Acquire mutex to protect shared resources */
  pthread_mutex_lock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);

  /* Stop T3492 timer if running */
  (void) esm_pt_stop_timer(user->esm_pt_data, pti);
  /* Set the procedure transaction state to INACTIVE */
  rc = esm_pt_set_status(user->esm_pt_data, pti, ESM_PT_INACTIVE);

  if (rc != RETURNok) {
    /* The procedure transaction was already in INACTIVE state
     * as the request may have already been rejected */
    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - PTI %d was already INACTIVE", pti);
    *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_MESSAGE_TYPE_NOT_COMPATIBLE;
    pthread_mutex_unlock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);
  } else {
    /* Release the transaction identity assigned to this procedure */
    rc = esm_pt_release(user->esm_pt_data, pti);

    if (rc != RETURNok) {
      LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-PROC  - Failed to release PTI %d", pti);
      *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_REQUEST_REJECTED_UNSPECIFIED;
      pthread_mutex_unlock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);
    } else if (*esm_cause != ESM_CAUSE_LAST_PDN_DISCONNECTION_NOT_ALLOWED) {
      /* Get the identity of the default EPS bearer context allocated to
       * the PDN connection entry assigned to this procedure transaction */
      int ebi = _pdn_disconnect_get_default_ebi(esm_data, pti);

      if (ebi < 0) {
        LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-PROC  - No default EPS bearer found");
        *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_PROTOCOL_ERROR;
        pthread_mutex_unlock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);
        LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
      }

      /*
       * Notify ESM that all EPS bearer contexts to this PDN have to be
       * locally deactivated
       */
      esm_sap_t esm_sap;
      esm_sap.primitive = ESM_EPS_BEARER_CONTEXT_DEACTIVATE_REQ;
      esm_sap.is_standalone = true;
      esm_sap.recv = NULL;
      esm_sap.send.length = 0;
      esm_sap.data.eps_bearer_context_deactivate.ebi = ebi;
      rc = esm_sap_send(user, &esm_sap);

      if (rc != RETURNok) {
        *esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_PROTOCOL_ERROR;
      }
      pthread_mutex_unlock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);
    } else {
      pthread_mutex_unlock(&pdn_disconnect_mutex);
    }
  }

  LOG_FUNC_RETURN(rc);
}"}   <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

{
  LOG_FUNC_IN;
  nas_user_t *user = args;
  esm_data_t *esm_data = user->esm_data;;
  int rc;

  /* Get retransmission timer parameters data */
  esm_pt_timer_data_t *data = (esm_pt_timer_data_t *)(args);

  /* Increment the retransmission counter */
